message:
feat(lua): implement lexical scoping via upvalue capture for Lua closures

Lua functions defined inside other functions could not access variables from
their enclosing scope because the evaluator always started with a fresh empty
Env. This broke standard Lua closure patterns commonly used in Redis scripts.

Now captures a snapshot of all lexical scopes at function definition time
(stored in LuaFunc.captured_env) and pre-loads them into the new Env when the
function is called, giving closures proper access to their defining environment.

- Add captured_env: Option<Vec<HashMap<String, LuaValue>>> to LuaFunc
- Add Env::snapshot() to capture current scope chain for upvalue storage
- Add Env::from_captured() to reconstruct scope chain from captured upvalues
- Modify function call dispatch to use captured env when available, pushing a
  fresh scope on top for function parameters/locals
- Apply to all three function definition paths: global, local, and anonymous

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
